I will rewrite Levels 1–5 for all 43 notes using the WIDER spread. The edits target the markdown files directly.

A Python script will handle all 43 files. Each level will follow its exact grade and style requirements.

I am now drafting the fresh level content for each note. I began with deliberate-practice, starting with bullet 1.

Drafted Levels 1–4 for deliberate-practice. Level 1 uses a concrete bike-wobble image. Level 2 compares deliberate practice to tuning one loose guitar string. Level 3 names the failing slice and links it to autopilot weakness. Level 4 instructs the reader to name the broken slice and drill it alone.

Finished deliberate-practice Level 5. It warns that comfortable reps signal drift and that deliberate practice means staying on what breaks until it holds.

Started develop-dont-endanger. Level 1 uses a coach ending practice when the field gets icy. Level 2 compares a drill to medicine. Level 3 names the failing slice and links it to autopilot weakness.

Finished develop-dont-endanger Levels 4–5. Level 4 asks the reader to name the skill a hard drill builds and swap the hazard if possible. Level 5 warns that intensity theater survives when Purpose and Vision are weak.

Started digital-garden. Level 1 uses a half-finished seed in a window box. Level 2 compares the garden to a backyard plot posted online.

Finished digital-garden Levels 3–5. Level 3 links notes to neighbors and feedback. Level 4 posts rough drafts and revises in public. Level 5 says public imperfection beats private hoarding.

Started digital-minimalism. Level 1 compares every new app to another hose in the yard.

Finished digital-minimalism Levels 2–5. Level 2 compares the app stack to a rattling kitchen drawer. Level 3 links maintenance to lost focus. Level 4 suggests cutting one app per week before adding another. Level 5 pairs Minimum Effective Dose with Digital Serendipity.

Started digital-serendipity. Bullet 1 defines serendipity as systems where old notes resurface at the right time.

Drafted Levels 1–4 for digital-serendipity. Level 1 uses a purpose-built shelf. Level 2 compares the system to a labeled junk drawer. Level 3 names the linked notes as the reason old ideas resurface. Level 4 instructs the reader to link each new note to two neighbors so forgotten ideas can surprise them later.

Finished digital-serendipity Level 5. It recommends a walkable graph over hoping for the right filename, citing Periodic Knowledge Review.

Started discern-his-will-daily. Level 1 uses the real-coin weight analogy. Level 2 compares obedience-first to learning a genuine scent before sorting fakes. Level 3 names the heart set on God's will as the filter that strips false teaching before it shapes choices.

Finished discern-his-will-daily Levels 4–5. Level 4 asks the reader to test a teaching by asking whether they would obey it if it cost them something today. Level 5 warns that without an obedience-first posture, clever teaching can sound like God while serving self, and cites Renewed Mind as the engine at the fork.

Started discipleship-vs-leadership. Level 1 compares leadership to saluting up while the squad watches for direction.

Finished discipleship-vs-leadership. Level 2 compares the two seats to one chair. Level 3 warns that swapping them lets public leadership substitute for private obedience. Level 4 instructs the reader to name which seat a decision serves before acting. Level 5 cites Discipleship for upward care and Leadership for outward care, noting that Servant Leadership overlaps horizontally but does not replace vertical allegiance. Started drafting the next note, discipleship.
